# Jeremie Louvaert — Full Context for LLMs

> Open-source developer specializing in ComfyUI custom node development. Founder of AKURATE.STUDIO, a premium brand intelligence platform. Based in Europe, building tools at the intersection of generative AI and production workflows since 2012 (GitHub member).

## About

Jeremie Louvaert builds production-grade open-source tooling for the ComfyUI ecosystem. ComfyUI is the leading node-based interface for running Stable Diffusion, SDXL, Flux, and other AI image/video generation models. His work focuses on solving practical problems that arise when moving from local GPU compute to cloud API-based workflows, on bringing conversational AI capabilities into node-based image editing, and on professional-grade color grading and film emulation for AI-generated imagery.

## Project: ComfyUI-Darkroom

Repository: https://github.com/jeremieLouvaert/ComfyUI-Darkroom
Language: Python
License: Open Source

### Problem Statement
The ComfyUI ecosystem has no professional-grade color grading or film emulation tools. Existing options are basic LUT loaders, simple grain overlays, or Instagram-filter-level color tools. Professional photographers and colorists have no way to apply real film stock characteristics, proper lens optical effects, or cinema-grade color processing within ComfyUI.

### Solution
A comprehensive suite of 11 nodes in two categories:

**Wave 1: Film Emulation Engine (6 nodes)**
1. Film Stock Color — 111 color film stocks across 7 categories (Negative, Slide, Cinema, FujiSim, Instant, Aged). Per-channel H&D characteristic curves with real Capture One data.
2. Film Stock B&W — 50 black & white stocks with spectral sensitivity coefficients from darktable + Capture One channel mix data.
3. Film Grain — Luminance-dependent grain simulation using OpenSimplex noise. Resolution-independent.
4. Halation — Light bouncing off film base. Soft threshold highlight extraction with screen blending.
5. Print Stock — Cinema print stock chain (Kodak 2383/2393, Fuji 3513/3510). Negative-to-print curve interaction.
6. Cross Process — C-41 in E-6 and E-6 in C-41 simulation.

**Wave 4: Lens & Optics (5 nodes)**
7. Chromatic Aberration — Lateral CA with quadratic radial profile. 102 real lens profiles.
8. Vignette — cos^4 physical falloff + mechanical vignetting. Anti-vignette correction mode.
9. Lens Distortion — Brown-Conrady model (k1/k2 radial coefficients). Barrel, pincushion, mustache.
10. Perspective Correct — Keystone + horizontal + rotation correction with auto-crop.
11. Lens Profile — Combined distortion + CA + vignette from real lens data.

### Key Technical Features
- 161 film stocks with real Capture One curve data (parsed from 586 .costyle XML files)
- 102 lens profiles across 10 brands: Canon, Nikon, Sony, Leica, Zeiss, Sigma, Fujifilm, Voigtlander, Vintage, Cinema
- Physics-based: H&D characteristic curves, cos^4 vignette law, Brown-Conrady distortion model
- Zero API costs — pure local computation
- Dependencies: scipy, opensimplex only

## Project: ComfyUI-Gemini-Direct

Repository: https://github.com/jeremieLouvaert/ComfyUI-Gemini-Direct
Language: Python
License: Open Source

### Problem Statement
Google Gemini’s native image generation is powerful but access through ComfyUI was limited to credit-based wrappers. Users needed a direct API integration that uses their own API key without intermediary billing.

### Solution
Two custom nodes:
1. Gemini Image Generate — Direct API call to Gemini image generation. Supports Gemini 2.0 Flash, 2.5 Flash, and 2.5 Pro models. Resolution and aspect ratio control. USD cost display per generation. Cache key output for Hash Vault compatibility.
2. Prompt Studio (AI Enhancer) — Domain-adaptive prompt expansion, refinement, and editing using Gemini text models. Anti-AI realism mandate built into system prompts.

## Project: ComfyUI-Gemini-Conversation-Canvas

Repository: https://github.com/jeremieLouvaert/ComfyUI-Gemini-Conversation-Canvas
Language: Python
License: MIT

### Problem Statement
Traditional image generation APIs (DALL-E, Midjourney, Flux) are single-shot: send a prompt, get an image, no memory between generations. Google Gemini’s native image generation is different — it maintains conversation context, remembering what it generated and reasoning about modifications. However, no ComfyUI node suite properly exploited this multi-turn capability.

### Solution
Five custom nodes that bring Gemini’s conversational image editing into ComfyUI:

1. Gemini Session (Start)
   - Creates a new conversation and generates the initial image
   - Supports text-to-image and image-to-image editing
   - Configurable model selection and aspect ratio

2. Gemini Edit Turn
   - Sends a natural language edit instruction to an existing conversation
   - Full scene coherence maintained across turns
   - Chainable — connect multiple in sequence for multi-step edits in one run
   - Optional reference image input per turn

3. Gemini Session (Save)
   - Persists conversation history and turn images to disk
   - API keys excluded from saved data for security
   - Concurrent-safe with FileLock + atomic writes

4. Gemini Session (Load)
   - Restores saved sessions for cross-run continuation
   - Rebuilds Gemini Chat object from serialized history with thought signature preservation

5. Gemini Session Gallery
   - Outputs all turn images as a batch tensor for visual comparison
   - Includes turn log with response text summaries

### Architecture
Session state flows between nodes as a Python dict containing serialized Gemini Content objects (via Pydantic model_dump). Images saved as separate PNGs to keep session JSON lightweight. Chat objects rebuilt from history using google-genai SDK’s chats.create(history=...) pattern.

### Key Technical Features
- Thought signature preservation for Gemini 3.x model compatibility
- History serialization/deserialization via Pydantic model_validate
- Concurrent-safe file operations with FileLock + atomic writes (temp file + os.replace)
- API key resolution: direct input > GEMINI_API_KEY env var > gemini_api_key.txt file
- ComfyUI IMAGE tensor ↔ PIL Image ↔ bytes conversion utilities

## Project: ComfyUI-Higgsfield-Direct

Repository: https://github.com/jeremieLouvaert/ComfyUI-Higgsfield-Direct
Language: Python
License: Open Source

### Problem Statement
Higgsfield.ai offers multiple image and video generation models (Soul 2.0, Seedream 4, Reve, Kling 2.1, Seedance, DOP) but had no ComfyUI integration.

### Solution
Four custom nodes:
1. Higgsfield Text-to-Image — Soul 2.0, Seedream 4, Reve models with resolution/aspect control and reference image support.
2. Higgsfield Image Edit — Seedream 4 Edit for text-guided image editing.
3. Higgsfield Image-to-Video — Kling 2.1 Pro, Seedance Pro, DOP Preview with 5/10/15s durations.
4. Higgsfield Model Info — Utility node listing available models and their capabilities.
Uses higgsfield-client SDK. API key via file/env/input.

## Project: ComfyUI-API-Optimizer

Repository: https://github.com/jeremieLouvaert/ComfyUI-API-Optimizer
Language: Python
License: Open Source

### Problem Statement
When ComfyUI workflows shift from local GPU inference to cloud APIs (Kling 3.0, Magnific, Banana.dev, RunPod), the bottlenecks change from VRAM to API costs, latency, and serialization. ComfyUI’s native caching often breaks with external API nodes due to dynamic timestamps and non-deterministic seeds.

### Solution
Three custom node types that integrate natively into ComfyUI:

1. API Cost & Quota Tracker
   - Circuit-breaker pattern for API spending
   - Persistent JSON ledger with per-provider cost tracking
   - Append-only JSONL transaction audit log with timestamps
   - Budget enforcement that halts execution before API charges
   - Uses decimal.Decimal for precise arithmetic (no floating-point drift)
   - Concurrent-safe with file locking

2. Deterministic Hash Vault (3 nodes)
   - Hash Vault Check: Hashes prompt text, parameters, and full tensor byte content
   - Lazy API Switch: Uses ComfyUI’s lazy evaluation to physically prevent upstream API node execution on cache hits
   - Hash Vault Save: Writes API outputs to disk cache with optional TTL
   - Features: recursive hashing for nested structures, device-portable caching, atomic writes

## Project: ComfyUI-Prompt-Vault

Repository: https://github.com/jeremieLouvaert/ComfyUI-Prompt-Vault
Language: Python
License: Open Source

### Problem Statement
AI image generation prompts for photorealism require specific camera, lighting, film stock, and composition terminology that most users don’t know. Generic prompt libraries are full of anime, influencer, and low-quality templates.

### Solution
Three custom nodes:
1. Prompt Vault Browse — 21 curated elite templates across 8 categories, filtered from 12,000+ raw prompts. Editable before output.
2. Prompt Vault Build — Assemble prompts from 100+ components: 27 cameras, 24 lighting setups, 18 film stocks, 15 moods, 7 quality directives, 10 compositions.
3. Prompt Vault Favorites — Persistent prompt library across sessions using JSON storage.
Zero dependencies. Curated exclusively for photorealism.

## Project: comfyui-wireless-link-simple

Repository: https://github.com/jeremieLouvaert/comfyui-wireless-link-simple
Language: Python
License: Free to use and modify

### Purpose
Minimal wireless data link implementation for ComfyUI, inspired by Blackmagic Fusion’s wireless links. Two nodes (Send/Get) with named channels and universal type support.
